channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: UK's Starmer welcomes German chancellor to his country estate as he touts 'reset' with the EU
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer welcomed German Chancellor Olaf Scholz to his country estate Sunday on the eve of seeking to improve relations with the European Union.
item.dc:creator: LONDON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's trade war among allies triggers retaliation from Canada and Mexico
item.description: Canada’s prime minister and Mexico’s president have ordered retaliatory tariffs against Americans, following through with their threats after U.S. Donald Trump sparked a trade war by imposing sweeping tariffs on imports from Canada, Mexico and China.
item.dc:creator: Rob Gillies And Maria Verza, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump says Americans could feel 'some pain' from his new tariffs that are triggering a trade war
item.description: President Donald Trump says Americans could feel “some pain” from the emerging trade war triggered by his tariffs against Canada, Mexico and China.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Jailed ex-Pakistani PM Imran Khan draws parallels to President Trump in fight for justice
item.description: Former Pakistani Prime Minister Imran Khan looms large over the country's political scene, despite being jailed. Now questions arise as to whether President Trump could bring him some relief.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China to file lawsuit with WTO, vows 'countermeasures' in response to Trump tariffs
item.description: China’s Ministry of Commerce said it would file a legal case against the United States at the World Trade Organization (WTO), in response to President Trump’s decision Saturday to impose 10 percent tariffs on Chinese goods.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: France braces for a political showdown as the new prime minister seeks to force a budget through
item.description: Political uncertainty is again looming in France after Prime Minister Francois Bayrou warned that he would use special executive powers this week to get his budget approved without a vote by lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu departs for US to meet with Trump, hoping to strengthen ties with Washington
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu left for the U.S. on Sunday to meet with U.S. President Donald Trump, looking to strengthen ties with Washington.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rubio visiting Central America with the Panama Canal and immigration top of mind
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — Secretary of State Marco Rubio is on his first foreign trip in office, arriving in Central America on Saturday to press President Donald Trump's top priority — curbing illegal immigration — and to bring the message that the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: King Charles says 'our hearts' are with United States after 'devastating loss of life' in DC plane crash
item.description: King Charles III said Saturday he was "shocked and saddened" about the deadly midair collision near Reagan National Airport Wednesday, saying his thoughts were with the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: USAID website goes offline in Trump administration's 2-week-old freeze on foreign aid worldwide
item.description: USAID’s website has dropped offline without explanation in the Trump administration's two-week-old funding freeze of U.S. foreign aid and development funding.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Knickmeyer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Gaza ceasefire sees its smoothest exchange yet of Israeli hostages for Palestinian prisoners
item.description: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip (AP) — The ceasefire in Gaza saw its smoothest exchange yet of Israeli hostages for Palestinian prisoners on Saturday, and the crucial Rafah border crossing reopened two days before discussions on the truce’s far more difficult second phase begin.
item.dc:creator: MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH and IMAD ISSEID, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: Hamas frees 3 hostages, Israel releases Palestinians as part of ceasefire deal
item.description: Hamas freed three hostages in the southern Gaza Strip on Saturday and Israel released 183 Palestinian prisoners in the fourth round of exchanges during the Gaza ceasefire deal between Israel and Hamas.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sick and wounded children begin crossing into Egypt as Rafah crossing opens after several months
item.description: RAFAH CROSSING, Egypt (AP) — A group of 50 sick and wounded Palestinian children began entering Egypt for treatment through Gaza’s Rafah crossing on Saturday, the first opening of the border since Israel captured it nearly nine months ago.
item.dc:creator: AHMED HATEM and AMR NABIL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Italy takes back 43 migrants it planned to process in Albania in 3rd failed attempt to keep them out
item.description: SHENGJIN, Albania (AP) — An Italian navy ship on Saturday took migrants to Italy from asylum processing centers in Albania following a court decision in Rome. It was the third failed attempt by Giorgia Meloni’s far-right government to process migrants in the non-EU country.
item.dc:creator: ERION XHABAFTI and LLAZAR SEMINI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Arab nations reject Trump’s suggestion to relocate Palestinians from Gaza to Egypt and Jordan
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Powerful Arab nations on Saturday rejected U.S. President Donald Trump’s suggestion to relocate Palestinians from Gaza to neighboring Egypt and Jordan.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Canadians largely against proposal to merge with US: Survey
item.description: The majority of Canadians are opposed to their country merging with the U.S., something President Trump has suggested in recent weeks, according to new surveys published on Friday. The new YouGov polls, conducted in both the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: 18 Pakistani soldiers killed in fighting with separatist rebels in Balochistan
item.description: QUETTA, Pakistan (AP) — Pakistani troops fought separatist insurgents who set up roadblocks in the restive northwestern region of Balochistan, leaving 18 paramilitary security forces and 23 rebels dead in some of the heaviest clashes in recent years, officials said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: ABDUL SATTAR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: American among latest hostage release in Gaza as Israel preps prisoner exchange
item.description: An Israeli American was among the latest batch of hostages released from Gaza during the temporary ceasefire between Israel and Palestinian militant group Hamas, according to officials. Hamas freed three hostages on Saturday, including Keith Siegel, 65, an American from North Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: As M23 rebel group advances in Congo, a new leader signals a shift in its identity
item.description: KAMPALA, Uganda (AP) — After Rwanda-backed M23 rebels took control of the biggest city in eastern Congo this week, the man who emerged from the shadows to assert his leadership was not the group's long-time military leader. Sultani Makenga, an ethnic Tutsi rebel leader sanctioned by both the U.S.
item.dc:creator: RODNEY MUHUMUZA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Peace prospects look bleak in Myanmar as a civil war rages
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Peace prospects look bleak in Myanmar as a civil war rages despite international pressure on the military four years after it seized power from an elected civilian government.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump refuses to say if he’s spoken to Putin; White House ‘talking’ about ending Ukraine war
item.description: President Trump on Friday wouldn’t say if he has spoken with Russian President Vladimir Putin since he began his second term but said there are ongoing discussions about ending the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Dutch police arrest three suspects after the theft of a priceless golden helmet from Romania
item.description: Three suspects have been arrested following the theft of an ancient golden helmet from Romania that had been on display in a museum in the Netherlands.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trudeau says Canada 'won't back down' as Trump ups tariff threat
item.description: Prime Minister Justin Trudeau said Canada “won’t back down” from President Trump’s threat to impose a 25 percent tariff and that Ottawa will respond accordingly until they are removed.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Trump's foreign aid freeze guts grassroots groups helping migrants in Latin America
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — A busy shelter for migrants in southern Mexico has been left without a doctor. A program to provide mental health support for LGBTQ+ youth fleeing Venezuela was disbanded.
item.dc:creator: MEGAN JANETSKY, MARIA VERZA and JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Grenell to give Venezuela a warning from Trump administration
item.description: President Trump’s envoy for special missions, Richard Grenell, is delivering an in-person message to Venezuelan leader Nicolás Maduro that there will be consequences if detained Americans are not released and Venezuelan “criminals and gang members” deported from the U.S. are not repatriated.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Turkey dismisses 5 military academy graduates for taking discontinued secular oath
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — Five military academy graduates and three of their immediate superiors were dismissed from the Turkish Armed Forces for taking a pro-secular oath during their graduation ceremony, the defense ministry announced on Friday The Ministry of National Defense launched a disciplinary
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia claims another Ukrainian village has fallen as it closes in on a key city
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia on Friday claimed it had captured another village in its relentless offensive in eastern Ukraine’s Donetsk region and as it closes in on the critical Ukrainian logistics hub of Pokrovsk after almost three years of war.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: First American hostage to be released from Gaza, Hamas says
item.description: Keith Segel, an American-Israeli in Gaza and the first of American citizens taken on Oct. 7, will be released on Saturday as part of the Israel-Hamas ceasfire deal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NASA's 2 stuck astronauts take their first spacewalk together
item.description: CAPE CANAVERAL, Fla. (AP) — NASA’s two stuck astronauts took their first spacewalk together Thursday, exiting the International Space Station almost eight months after moving in.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: An Iraqi man who carried out Quran burnings in Sweden is killed in a shooting
item.description: Salwan Momika, a 38-year-old Iraqi man who staged several burnings and desecrations of the Quran in Sweden in 2023, has been killed in a shooting near Stockholm.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Caribbean nation of St Kitts launches investigation after 19 people found dead in drifting vessel
item.description: Authorities in the Caribbean nation of St. Kitts and Nevis have launched an investigation after discovering a partially submerged boat containing at least 19 bodies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Passenger plane catches fire at South Korean airport; all 176 people on board are evacuated
item.description: A passenger plane operated by Air Busan caught fire before takeoff at an airport in South Korea; all 176 people on board were safely evacuated using an escape slide.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korean leader Kim inspects nuclear facility as Pyongyang pressures Trump administration
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korean leader Kim Jong Un inspected a facility that produces nuclear material and called for bolstering the country’s nuclear fighting capability, state media reported Wednesday, as the North looks to increase pressure on the United States following the inauguration o
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and FOSTER KLUG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: CDC ordered to stop working with WHO immediately, upending expectations of an extended withdrawal
item.description: President Donald Trump last week issued an executive order to begin the process of withdrawing the U.S. from WHO, but that did not take immediate effect.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Preliminary report on South Korean plane crash confirms bird strike, but no cause yet
item.description: The preliminary accident report released by South Korea's Aviation and Railway Accident Investigation Board on Monday said that feathers and bird blood stains were found in both engines.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-jin Kim, WGN 9
